projects.organization: LIRA – Laboratorio de Investigación y Robótica Avanzada UNAM
highlights: As part of the lunar rover team, I developed ROS2 nodes for robotic arm control, enabling navigation, manipulation, and task execution, while designing and implementing control logic for both autonomous and semi-autonomous arm operations. My work focused on automating a robotic manipulation system using a xArm6 manipulator within the ROS2 environment, integrating a computer vision pipeline with YOLOv8 and Intel RealSense cameras for target detection and spatial estimation. For manipulator control, I developed motion planning nodes based on algorithmic path generation, implementing the RRT (Rapidly-exploring Random Tree) algorithm to compute efficient, collision-free trajectories, ensuring a fully automated, safe, and optimized process during tracking and collection tasks. This work was showcased at a lunar rover competition held in Puebla, April 2026.
Led the development of a mobile application prototype for Android using Kotlin.
Applied Clean Architecture principles for scalability and maintainability.
Implemented data extraction from an external API with transformation and debugging pipeline.
